title: cynoglossum anchusoides subsp. asperum
intro: Cynoglossum anchusoides subsp. asperum, also known as the Alpine Hound's Tongue, is a perennial plant in the borage family native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits associated with Cynoglossum anchusoides subsp. asperum include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made from its leaves and flowers, which are often dried and used in teas or combined with other herbs to create medicinal remedies.
